decision: When no X-Forwarded-User header is present, requireAuth/requireAuthOrThrow treat the request as a trusted local operator unless DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH=1 forces strict 401 enforcement.
rationale: Testcontainers (and other guarded routes) returned 401 in host dev mode and proxy-less Tailscale container runs because only the Pocket ID proxy injects the header; operator approved a default-bypass gate so the app is usable without a proxy while remaining lockable behind one.
alternatives: Bypass only when NODE_ENV!=production (rejected: Tailscale container is a production build with no proxy, so it would still 401); always require auth (rejected: blocks local/proxy-less use).

decision: Add requireAuth to the four mutating testcontainers compose routes (POST create, DELETE, start, stop); leave GET list unauthenticated like other read-only GETs.
rationale: Compose mutation routes previously had no auth guard at all, inconsistent with the per-container routes.
alternatives: Leave compose routes open (rejected: inconsistent and unguarded mutation).

decision: Refine the auth bypass to distinguish an ABSENT forward-auth user header from a PRESENT-but-empty one. requireAuth/requireAuthOrThrow bypass to LOCAL_OPERATOR only when the raw X-Forwarded-User header is truly absent (null) and DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH!=1; a present-but-empty header (e.g. X-Forwarded-User: "") is treated as a malformed credential and returns 401 / throws even when DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH is unset.
rationale: Per auth-gate-001 the bypass keyed on getAuthUser().authenticated===false, which conflated 'no header present' with 'header present but empty'. A client able to reach the app directly and send an empty X-Forwarded-User could trigger the local-operator bypass, contradicting the 'no header present' policy. Reading the raw header (no || coercion) and bypassing only on null closes that gap while preserving proxy-less host/Tailscale use.
alternatives: Leave bypass keyed on authenticated===false (rejected: empty-string header bypasses); also trim/treat whitespace-only as invalid (deferred: whitespace yields an authenticated user via getAuthUser and is a separate latent issue, out of scope for this change).

decision: Trim the forward-auth user header and treat an empty-or-whitespace-only X-Forwarded-User as an invalid credential. getAuthContext() computes userId = (rawUserHeader ?? "").trim() || null, so a present-but-empty or whitespace-only header yields an unauthenticated user; requireAuth/requireAuthOrThrow return 401 / throw even when DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH is unset. A truly ABSENT header (rawUserHeader === null) still bypasses to LOCAL_OPERATOR when DAAX_REQUIRE_AUTH!=1. Supersedes auth-gate-003's 'alternatives' note that deferred whitespace handling as out of scope: whitespace-only is now in scope and rejected.
rationale: auth-gate-003 closed the present-but-empty-string gap but explicitly deferred whitespace-only (a value like '   ' was truthy in getAuthUser and produced an authenticated user with a whitespace username). A later Copilot review brought whitespace into scope as the same class of malformed credential, so getAuthContext now trims before validating. This also unified the header read into a single headers() lookup per request (getAuthContext returns { rawUserHeader, user }; isUserHeaderAbsent removed), with the guards deciding bypass on rawUserHeader === null.
alternatives: Reject whitespace only in the guards rather than getAuthUser (rejected: getAuthUser would still report authenticated=true for a whitespace identity, leaving a latent inconsistency); leave whitespace as deferred per 003 (rejected: contradicts the shipped lib/auth.ts behavior and the decision record).
